title: Carissa campenonii
intro: Carissa carolinensis, also known as the num-num or Carissa, is a flowering plant native to southern Africa. The num-num has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Carissa carolinensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from the num-num include teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as extracts and tinctures used in traditional medicine and complementary therapies.
taxonomy: Carissa campenonii is classified as a species within the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. It is a genus of flowering plants native to southern Africa. This evergreen shrub or small tree is a member of the subfamily Rauvolfioideae. Within the genus Carissa, C. campenonii is one of several species found in southern Africa. The classification of Carissa campenonii reflects its botanical and evolutionary relationships to other plants within the Apocynaceae family.
